MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File Third Amended Supplemental Complt
Off Calendar Oct-19-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Pamela Pitt, Esq., 415-291-9251 for plaintiff and moving party Claudia Carter

Alison Dibley, Esq., of Jackson & Lewis, 415-394-9400 for defendant and opposing party San Francisco Unified School District.

RULING:
MATTER WAS NOT A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows --
PLAINTIFF CLAUDIA CARTER'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File Third Amended Supplemental Complaint IS GRANTED ON CONDITION THAT PLAINTIFF NOT OPPOSE REQUEST BY DEFENDANT FOR A REASONABLE TRIAL CONTINUANCE TO ACCOMODATE ADDITIONAL DISCOVERY AND MOTION PRACTICE RESPONSIVE TO THE AMENDMENTS. RESPONDENT'S COUNSEL REQUESTED 30 DAYS TO RESPOND TO AMENDED COMPLAINT FROM DATE OF SERVICE OF FILING. THE COURT GRANTED THE REQUEST.
Prevailing party to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189. (301/PJB/CK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Crc Rule 335
Off Calendar Jul-20-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Isaac P. Hernandez, for Defendant/Opposing party

Ruling:
No other appearance. Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part. As to Nos. 7, 8, 9, and 12, the motion is granted as the objections are overruled. The response otherwise may be adequate.
As to Nos. 10, 11, 13, and 14, the objections overruled. Defendant is ordered to respond further.
As to Nos. 16, 17, and 18, the motion is granted as the objections are overruled. As Defendant has obtained further information after further investigation, Defendant shall supplement the responses with any later obtained information.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
